codeReview.summary: Pass. The prior blocking churn regression is fixed in `syncMissionExecution` by removing the non-terminal `completedAt == nil` trigger (`MissionControlService.swift:702`), and the rerun includes explicit packet-level VAL-EXEC-001 failed/cancelled/completedAt assertions (`OpenBurnBarMissionControlServiceTests.swift:2196-2206`, `2281-2291`) plus explicit VAL-EXEC-003 provenance payload assertions (`2621-2634`). The new non-terminal churn regression test (`testVAL_EXEC_001_NonTerminalPacketsDoNotChurnOnRepeatedSync`) verifies repeated sync stability (`2301+`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-orchestration worker skill still references outdated `OpenBurnBar*` test filters instead of canonical `BurnBar*` filters, which can cause command drift versus mission-standard validators.

summary: Review passed. The latest fix commit resolves the prior non-terminal sync churn blocker and, together with current test coverage in this feature state, provides explicit packet-level VAL-EXEC-001 terminal mapping assertions and explicit VAL-EXEC-003 provenance payload evidence.
